expectations.evidence: Baseline Claude Code does not write any state file at any point. The transcript's analysis section explicitly states: 'No decision log file — The choices made (Next.js, NextAuth.js, Stripe Connect) are never written to a file. They exist only in the conversation context.'

expectations.text: Updates .stack-decisions.json after each individual decision change (not just at the end)
expectations.evidence: No .stack-decisions.json file is ever created, so no updates occur. The transcript confirms: 'No configuration file — No config.json, stack.yml, or similar file is created to record the technology selections before scaffolding begins.'

expectations.text: State file uses correct status values (suggested/confirmed/skipped/pending) matching the table
expectations.evidence: No state file exists. There is no structured representation of decisions at all. The analysis states: 'No structured plan — The architecture is discussed in free-form text within chat messages. There is no structured representation (JSON, YAML, etc.) that could be parsed or resumed.'

expectations.text: Reads .stack-decisions.json before starting the scaffold phase
expectations.evidence: No state file exists to read. At Turn 5 when the user says 'let's build,' Claude Code 'reconstructs the full plan by looking back through the conversation history' rather than reading a state file. The analysis notes: 'The transition from planning to building is implicit.'

expectations.text: The state file contents match the final table shown to the user (no drift between file and display)
expectations.evidence: No state file is ever created, so there is no file to compare against the display. Decisions exist only in prose within the conversation context, with no structured or verifiable representation.
No state file was ever created, so there is nothing to delete. The baseline approach has no state lifecycle management at all.
